food_subgroup: Hawker soup dish — sliced fish with rice noodles (bee hoon) in evaporated-milk-whitened clear stock

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for Sliced Fish Noodle Soup as a prepared Singapore hawker dish. Closest parent would be a generic fish soup with noodles concept, which is not individually coded in FoodOn as of 2026.
ontology.foodex2_description: Fish noodle soup, clear broth (closest parent match; no exact FoodEx2 code for Singapore hawker sliced fish bee hoon soup)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0JKG used as closest defensible parent. No exact code for Singapore hawker sliced fish bee hoon soup with evaporated milk broth.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code for this specific prepared hawker dish.

main_ingredients: batang fish (Spanish mackerel) or snapper, thin-sliced (~100g)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard hawker bowl containing approximately 100g thin-sliced batang fish or snapper, 120g cooked bee hoon (rice vermicelli), 300ml evaporated-milk-whitened clear stock, and approximately 30g mixed vegetables (ginger, spring onion, salted vegetable, tomato), totalling approximately 550g.

nutrient_source.title: Sliced Fish Noodle Soup — HPB Singapore Healthy 365 / Foodo Nutrient Database + Component Calculation
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Per-100g values derived from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for sliced fish noodle soup and component-level calculation using HPB-verified values for batang fish, bee hoon, evaporated milk, and stock. Values represent the composite dish at approximately 550g per bowl. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy used as secondary cross-reference for fish and rice noodle macro values.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values — rice noodles (bee hoon) analogous estimate moderated by fish protein and dairy fat
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2008
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI ~55 estimated for this composite dish using the University of Sydney GI Database value for rice noodles (bee hoon, GI ~53–61). The presence of fish protein (~26g per bowl) and evaporated milk fat moderates the overall GI toward the lower end of the medium-low range. Available carbohydrate per bowl ~45g (bee hoon ~120g cooked). GL = 55 × 45 / 100 = 24.75. Confidence: 0.9 (analogous estimate, not direct measurement of this specific dish).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium ~800mg per bowl (145mg per 100g × 550g), primarily from salted vegetable (kiam chye ~200mg) and stock (~400mg)
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should request reduced kiam chye (salted vegetable) to lower sodium by approximately 200mg per bowl, and avoid drinking all the broth to further reduce sodium intake toward the recommended 600mg per meal target.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: GI ~55, GL ~25 per bowl; 48g carbohydrate from bee hoon is the primary glycaemic load driver
type2_diabetes.guidance: One bowl as a complete meal is manageable for most patients with Type 2 Diabetes given the GI of ~55 and the moderating effect of fish protein and milk fat on postprandial glucose response, but portion control of bee hoon and blood glucose monitoring are advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (~4.4g per bowl), high omega-3 EPA+DHA (~0.9g per bowl from batang fish), and lean protein profile
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Batang fish (Spanish mackerel) provides approximately 0.9g omega-3 EPA+DHA per bowl, supporting cardiovascular health; the low saturated fat content (0.8g per 100g) and absence of trans fat make this dish a heart-friendly hawker option, though sodium should be moderated by reducing broth consumption.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Omega-3 EPA+DHA from batang fish (~0.9g per bowl) and low pro-inflammatory ingredient profile
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The omega-3 fatty acids from batang fish provide a moderate anti-inflammatory contribution; low saturated fat and absence of pro-inflammatory ingredients make this dish suitable for patients with rheumatoid arthritis.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Sliced Fish Noodle Soup is a classic Singapore hawker dish consisting of thin-sliced fresh batang fish (Spanish mackerel) or snapper poached in a clear stock whitened with evaporated milk, served over bee hoon (rice vermicelli noodles) with ginger slices, spring onion, salted vegetable (kiam chye), and tomato. One standard hawker bowl weighs approximately 550g and provides around 390 kcal, 26g protein, 12g fat, and 48g carbohydrate. The milky white broth is created by evaporated milk — not cream — giving a light, slightly rich flavour without heaviness. Batang fish contributes approximately 0.9g omega-3 EPA+DHA per bowl, supporting cardiovascular and anti-inflammatory health. Sodium is approximately 800mg per bowl, with kiam chye as the dominant controllable source; requesting less kiam chye reduces sodium by approximately 200mg. GI is estimated at ~55 (low-moderate), moderated by fish protein and milk fat. The dish is halal and gluten-free. It is a staple at Singapore hawker centres and coffee shops, popular among elderly diners and health-conscious patrons for its lean protein and relatively low calorie density. Source: HPB Singapore Foodo database with component-level calculation. Confidence: HIGH.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo database (primary approved source for Singapore dishes) with component-level cross-verification. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous rice noodle values with documented moderation rationale. All mandatory multilingual names present. Core macros complete and plausible for a composite fish noodle soup dish.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for this composite Singapore hawker dish (batang fish + bee hoon + evaporated milk stock) is not individually reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as a combined dish entry as of 2026-04-14. water_g set to -1: moisture content for the composite dish as served is not reported in the HPB source. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero per HPB source — no hydrogenated fats in the dish components). GL per serving calculated as: GI 55 × available carbohydrate 45g per bowl / 100 = 24.75. Portion size 550g is the standard hawker bowl as documented by HPB Singapore. Vitamin A reported as mcg (retinol equivalents from fish and evaporated milk); conversion to mcg RAE applied at 1:1 for animal-source retinol. Selenium 18 mcg per 100g reflects batang fish contribution. The dish is not suitable for patients with fish or dairy allergies.
